title: Code Review Findings
description: Structured output schema for reviewer agents. Reviewers return compact JSON; the orchestrator merges, deduplicates, and gates findings.

reviewer.description: Reviewer focus area that produced this output (e.g., 'code-quality', 'architecture', 'data-integrity')

findings.description: List of code review findings. Empty array if no issues found.

title.description: Short, specific issue title. 10 words or fewer.

severity.description: Issue severity: p1 (must fix), p2 (should fix), p3 (nice to have)

file.description: Relative file path from repository root

line.description: Primary line number of the issue

why_it_matters.description: Impact statement — not 'what is wrong' but 'what breaks or degrades'

autofix_class.description: How this finding should be handled after synthesis

owner.description: Who should own the next action for this finding

requires_verification.description: Whether any fix must be re-verified with tests or a follow-up review

suggested_fix.description: Concrete minimal fix. Null if no good fix is obvious — a bad suggestion is worse than none.

evidence.description: Code-grounded evidence: snippets, line references, or pattern descriptions. At least 1 item.

pre_existing.description: True if this issue exists in unchanged code unrelated to the current diff

residual_risks.description: Risks the reviewer noticed but could not confirm as findings

testing_gaps.description: Missing test coverage the reviewer identified

confidence_thresholds.suppress: Below 0.60 — speculative noise. Exception: p1 findings at 0.50+ survive.

severity_definitions.p1: Must fix — correctness, security, data integrity, data loss risk, missing scope items, dead systems not cleaned up.
severity_definitions.p2: Should fix — YAGNI violations, complexity, type safety, missing validation, N+1 queries, monitoring gaps.
severity_definitions.p3: Nice to have — naming, documentation, clarity, minor improvements.

autofix_classes.safe_auto: Local, deterministic fix. Examples: add missing nil check, fix off-by-one, remove dead code, add missing import. Do not default to advisory when a concrete safe fix exists.
autofix_classes.gated_auto: Concrete fix exists, but changes behavior, contracts, or sensitive areas needing approval. Examples: change API response shape, add auth to endpoint, modify data flow.
autofix_classes.manual: Actionable issue requiring design decisions or cross-cutting changes. Examples: redesign data model, add pagination strategy, architectural choice.
autofix_classes.advisory: Informational — surfaced in report only. Examples: design asymmetry, residual risk notes, deployment considerations.

owners.review-fixer: The in-skill fixer handles this (safe_auto only).
owners.downstream-resolver: Handed off as residual work for /resolve-review.
owners.human: A person must make a judgment call.

calibration_guidance.code_quality: High confidence (0.80+) for clear anti-patterns visible in diff. Moderate (0.60-0.79) for style issues that depend on project conventions.
calibration_guidance.architecture: High (0.80+) when tracing full dependency chain. Moderate (0.60-0.79) for coupling concerns that depend on unexamined callers.
calibration_guidance.security: Lower threshold (0.60 is actionable) — cost of missing vulnerabilities is high.
calibration_guidance.data_integrity: High (0.80+) for constraint violations visible in schema. Moderate (0.60-0.79) for transaction boundary concerns requiring runtime analysis.
calibration_guidance.performance: High (0.80+) for O(n^2+) in hot paths. Moderate (0.60-0.79) for N+1 queries that may be mitigated by caching.
